name: Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Palacios del Pan
name_original: Embalse de Ricobayo — vuelta desde Palacios de Pan
description: Explore the Embalse de Ricobayo on this easy 13.8-mile (22.2 km) mountain biking loop from Palacios de Pan, offering scenic reservoir views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Palacios Del Pan, located in the Tierra del Pan region of Zamora, Spain, offers diverse landscapes centered on the Ricobayo Reservoir. The area features varied terrain, including the expansive reservoir shores, the agroforestry system of Dehesa de Mázares, and the striking Peñas Rojas. Rural roads and paths wind through cereal cultivation areas, providing a serene setting for cycling. The region's hydrology is enhanced by several streams belonging to the Duero basin. Best mountain bike trails around Palacios Del Pan The most popular mountain bike trail is Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail, a 32.9 miles (52.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers extensive views of the Ricobayo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Palacios del Pan, an easy 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding rural landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Perilla de Castro – Losacino Bridge loop from Carbajales de Alba, a 39.5 miles (63.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 42 minutes. Mountain biking around Palacios Del Pan is defined by the Ricobayo Reservoir, Dehesa de Mázares, and rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Palacios Del Pan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Palacios Del Pan?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 10 mountain bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Palacios Del Pan listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes centered around the Ricobayo Reservoir and the surrounding rural areas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Palacios Del Pan?
A: Yes, there are options for beginners. An excellent easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018301714/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-from-palacios-del-pan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Palacios del Pan&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.8 miles (22.2 km) long and offers scenic views of the reservoir and rural landscapes without significant technical challenges.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Palacios Del Pan?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the area offers several difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perilla de Castro – Losacino Bridge loop from Carbajales de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, a substantial 39.5 miles (63.5 km) trail that leads through varied and demanding terrain.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Palacios Del Pan?
A: Mountain biking in Palacios Del Pan offers stunning natural scenery. You'll primarily encounter the expansive &lt;strong&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, with its picturesque views. The routes also traverse the &lt;strong&gt;Dehesa de Mázares&lt;/strong&gt;, an agroforestry system, and may offer glimpses of the striking &lt;strong&gt;Peñas Rojas&lt;/strong&gt; (Red Rocks). The region's cereal cultivation areas provide a serene rural backdrop.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in points of interest. You can encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6118308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Manzanal Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which crosses the reservoir. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; and various historical religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4835252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real de La Hiniesta&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3024773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find loop mountain bike trails in Palacios Del Pan?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Palacios Del Pan are designed as loops, making them convenient for starting and ending at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that circles the reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Palacios Del Pan?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Palacios Del Pan, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite warm, while winters are cooler but still suitable for riding, though you should check local weather forecasts.

Q: Are there any places to relax or swim near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, along the shores of the Ricobayo Reservoir, you can find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pilares del Pan&lt;/a&gt;. This beach area is equipped with showers and grassy spots, providing a perfect place to relax or even take a dip after your ride, especially during warmer months.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Palacios Del Pan is varied, offering a mix of experiences. You'll encounter paths ('senda'), narrower trails ('sendero estrecho'), and rural roads. This diversity ensures an engaging biking experience, from smooth sections to more technical stretches, often winding through agricultural lands and along the reservoir's edge.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore while mountain biking?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the region holds historical significance. You might encounter the ruins of &lt;strong&gt;Casetón de los Moros&lt;/strong&gt; in the Dehesa de Mázares, which contain vestiges of Roman constructions. The impressive &lt;strong&gt;Martín Gil Viaduct&lt;/strong&gt;, an engineering marvel from 1942, is also a significant historical landmark in the broader area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Palacios Del Pan?
A: Mountain bikers frequently praise the extensive views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and the serene, expansive rural setting. The variety of terrain, from easy loops to more challenging routes, is also highly appreciated, allowing riders of all levels to find suitable adventures.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly good views of the Ricobayo Reservoir?
A: Many routes in the area offer excellent views of the Ricobayo Reservoir, as it's a central feature of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its extensive reservoir vistas throughout the ride.
tips: Just 2 km from the town of Palacios del Pan and towards Carbajales, we have the detour to the Peñas Rojas and El Madrigal urbanizations, following the path that takes us to the Club de Vela Zamora and without turning off to said urbanizations we access the beach that has showers, grass areas, without a doubt, a place of enjoyment and peace in this part of the Ricobayo reservoir.
Bridge over the mouth of the reservoir, the Valdelasmozas Bridge has a firm narrow gravel.

faqs.question: Where does the mountain biking route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This route, "Embalse de Ricobayo — vuelta desde Palacios de Pan," begins and ends in the village of Palacios de Pan. While specific parking areas aren't designated, you can typically find roadside parking within the village, especially near the main square or residential streets, as it's a small community.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter local roads with light traffic, along with numerous tracks (pistas) and trails (senderos). Expect undulating sections through cereal fields, pastures, and holm oak forests. Some parts near the water's edge may be unpaved, alternating between jara plants, holm oaks, and open fields, generally with gentle ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views of the Ricobayo Dam and the expansive reservoir. You'll also pass by interesting points such as the Pilares del Pan Beach and the Valdelasmozas Bridge. The area is known for its natural beauty, including the "Arribes del Esla," and you might even spot historical ruins like those of Castrotorafe in the broader region.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific Komoot route is classified as easy, making it suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. The elevation gain is moderate, and the terrain, while varied, doesn't present significant technical challenges, allowing for an enjoyable experience around the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de Ricobayo — vuelta desde Palacios de Pan trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or on roads with light traffic. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as water sources might be limited.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Spring also brings blooming landscapes, while autumn offers beautiful colors.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride this mountain biking route. It traverses public roads and tracks around the Embalse de Ricobayo, which are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with a few other paths. You'll find short sections that coincide with the Camino Portugués de la vía de la Plata, the Mtb Camino Portugués de la vía de la Plata, and the Camino del Monte.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trailhead in Palacios de Pan?
faqs.answer: Palacios de Pan is a small village, so dining options might be limited directly at the trailhead. It's advisable to bring your own snacks and drinks for the ride. For more extensive options, you might need to visit larger nearby towns before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: For this easy mountain biking route, pack essentials like plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump), sun protection (sunscreen, hat), and appropriate cycling attire. Given the varied terrain, comfortable mountain biking shoes are recommended. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies.

faqs.question: Are there any specific considerations for riding near the Embalse de Ricobayo?
faqs.answer: When riding near the Embalse de Ricobayo, be mindful of the changing terrain, which can include unpaved sections close to the water. Also, be aware that while the route is easy, the broader area can have more challenging trails, so stick to the planned route if you're looking for a relaxed ride. Always respect private property and agricultural lands.
